A daughter who would not be a daughter, a knight who would not be a subject. The Spire has no use for crowns — but it does enjoy a good betrayal.
Climb, then, Knight of Treachery. Tear off as many helms as you like up there. The floors above kneel to no one either.
Back again, chasing acknowledgment from stone? It will not call you son, nor king. It will only count your dead.
Draw Clarent if it pleases you. I have outlasted Camelot and the king who built it; your lightning is a candle to me.
